facet: idle-relayed memory wall (relay RSS per client connection)
driver: Claude Code on the Mac over SSH
topology: single-box, co-located (relay + responder + generator all on node-1)
co_location_caveat: Generator co-located with the relay. This understates the NIC/throughput wall (loopback), but the IDLE-CONNECTION MEMORY wall measured here is valid: per-connection relay RSS does not depend on where the generator runs. Cross-host NIC/CPU walls and the matchmaking cost gap require the SG widening + the NAT'd Mac.

relay.isolation: systemd transient unit relay-e0.service, MemoryMax=4G, CPUQuota=200% (2 vCPU)
relay.tls: self-signed (iroh_relay testing certs); clients use CaTlsConfig::insecure_skip_verify (lab-only)

generator.mode: passthrough (forced relay-only via clear_ip_transports per endpoint)
generator.unit: one independent endpoint (own node key) per connection = one relay client connection

method: Relay in a 4G/2-vCPU cgroup slice. Baseline RSS = relay + 1 responder connection. For each N, bring up N forced-relay endpoints, confirm establishment via the generator's ESTABLISHED marker, sample relay cgroup memory.current (median of 3 samples 2s apart) at steady state, drop, repeat. Marginal per-conn = slope across N (cancels fixed process overhead).
measurement_signal: cgroup memory.current of the relay-only cgroup (relay process RSS; generator + responder run outside the slice)

result.finding: ~31 KiB marginal RSS per idle relayed client connection on iroh-relay 1.0.0 — well BELOW the 50-150 KiB hypothesis. Linear in N (per-conn stable 30.5-32.4 KiB across the sweep). A 4 GiB relay slice => ~130k idle relayed connections on the memory ceiling alone (other ceilings bind sooner under active load).
